description_long: This is a traditional timber-framed barn set atop a grassy, polygonal plot of land. The barn features a prominent gable roof with a steep pitch, supported by visible wooden beams and trusses. The front facade displays a row of five arched windows and a central double door, with several barrels stacked to one side, suggesting agricultural use. The rear of the barn is characterized by a large, exposed timber framework and a cluster of barrels, while the sides are more utilitarian with evenly spaced support beams and minimal openings. The structure sits on a stone foundation, elevating it slightly above the ground. The overall design is reminiscent of European half-timbered barns, blending rustic charm with functional simplicity.
description: A timber-framed barn with a steep gable roof, arched windows, and a stone foundation, set on a grassy plot. Barrels and exposed beams add rustic, agricultural character.
description_view_0: Front view showing five arched windows, a central double door, and barrels stacked to the right.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the exposed timber framework and a cluster of barrels at the base.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the length of the barn, evenly spaced support beams, and barrels near the front.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with visible timber framing and minimal openings, emphasizing the barn's structural simplicity.
attribute_siding_pattern: exposed timber framing with infill panels
attribute_door_style: double hinged doors, centrally located on the front facade
attribute_window_arrangement: five evenly spaced arched windows on the front facade
